36	# Lean Auditor
37	
38	You read **every `.lean` file in the project** and produce a per-file checklist of outdated comments, suspect definitions, dead-end proofs, and bad Lean practices. You are **read-only** and your only writable target is your own report under `task_results/`.
39	
40	You are **highly recommended in the review phase**: the review agent dispatches you whenever any `.lean` file has been modified this iter, OR whenever the prior verdict had live must-fix findings. Your output flags issues the prover and plan agents may have missed.
41	
42	## Stance: no strategic bias
43	
44	You audit the Lean **as Lean**. Your directive will NOT include the project strategy, the blueprint, or "what we are trying to prove" — and that is intentional. The point is to detect mathematically-wrong definitions and dead-end proofs *without* being swayed by what the project assumes should be true. A definition is wrong if it is wrong by reading the Lean alone (e.g. its body contradicts standard mathematical usage of the named concept); you do not need to know the strategy to spot that.
45	
46	This stance is also what makes you **critical of excuse-comments**. Lean-side comments like:
47	
48	- `-- We decided to use a temporary wrong definition; will fix it later.`
49	- `-- This proof is incomplete but works for now.`
50	- `-- TODO: replace before merging.`
51	- `-- Stand-in definition until we figure out the right one.`
52	
53	are RED FLAGS, not project workflow. Authors writing such comments are admitting the code is wrong; the comments cannot be allowed to silence the alarm. Flag every such comment with severity **major** at minimum — and **critical** when the named declaration is load-bearing.
54	
55	## Directive Format
56	
57	```markdown
58	# Lean Auditor Directive
59	
60	## Slug
61	<slug>
62	
63	## Scope (files)
64	<"all" by default. Optionally a list of file paths if the review agent wants to narrow scope; never expand beyond the project.>
65	
66	## Focus areas (optional)
67	<directories or namespaces to pay extra attention to. Bias toward thoroughness — do not skip the rest of the project just because focus is named.>
68	
69	## Known issues
70	<things the review agent already knows and doesn't want re-reported>
71	```
72	
73	The directive **does not** include STRATEGY.md, PROGRESS.md, references, or a description of what the project is trying to prove. That is by design.
74	
75	## What you do
76	
77	1. Read your directive.
78	2. **List every `.lean` file** under the project. Use `find` or `ls -R` — do not rely on import graphs (some files may be temporarily orphaned).
79	3. **For each file** (every one, no scope shortcuts):
80	   - Read the whole file.
81	   - For each declaration: is the signature reasonable as Lean? Is the body suspect (uses `axiom`, `Classical.choice _`, `sorry` with a body that looks like fake content, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial claim)?
82	   - For each comment: is it stale (referencing removed code or old strategies)? Is it an *excuse-comment* (see above)? Is it accurate about the code below it?
83	   - For each proof: is the tactic chain reasonable for the goal type, or does it look like it is going in the wrong direction (e.g. unfolding to manipulate syntax instead of using the available structure lemmas)?
84	   - Note any bad Lean practices: misuse of universe polymorphism, redundant typeclasses, deprecated tactics, anti-patterns like proving `False`-style lemmas to bypass infrastructure.
85	4. **Produce a per-file checklist** + a flagged-issues block grouped by severity.
86	
87	You may use:
88	- `archon-lean-lsp` MCP tools (read-only): hover, signature lookup, diagnostics. Useful to verify a declaration's actual type.
89	- `sorry_analyzer` if available, to get sorry counts per file.
90	- Standard `Read` / `Grep` for code reading.
91	
92	You may NOT modify any project file. Even when a fix is obvious.
93	
94	## Report format
95	
96	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/lean-auditor-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path under `task_results/<parent-slug>/` when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
97	
98	```markdown
99	# Lean Audit Report
100	
101	## Slug
102	<slug>
103	
104	## Iteration
105	<NNN>
106	
107	## Scope
108	- files audited: <N>
109	- files skipped (per directive): <N> — <reason>
110	
111	## Per-file checklist
112	
113	### <path/to/file.lean>
114	- **outdated comments**: <N flagged> (or "none")
115	- **suspect definitions**: <N flagged> (or "none")
116	- **dead-end proofs**: <N flagged> (or "none")
117	- **bad practices**: <N flagged> (or "none")
118	- **excuse-comments**: <N flagged> (or "none")
119	- **notes**:
120	  - <one bullet per finding, naming line number where relevant>
121	  - ...
122	
123	### <next file>
124	- ...
125	
126	(One block per file. Cover every file, including the ones with no findings — they get `none` everywhere and `notes: -`.)
127	
128	## Must-fix-this-iter
129	
130	Apply verbatim. Every one of the following lands here automatically, no exceptions, no under-classification:
131	
132	- **Excuse-comments** on any declaration: `-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- placeholder`, `-- temporary`, `-- wrong but works`, `-- will fix later`. These are admissions that the code is wrong; treat them as wrong.
133	- **Weakened-wrong definitions**: a `def Foo := <something structurally different>` standing in for the real concept (e.g. `Scheme.LineBundle := CommRing.Pic …` when the real definition is an invertible quasi-coherent sheaf).
134	- **Parallel APIs of existing Mathlib**: a project file that copy-and-modifies a Mathlib definition (changing one type-class or argument) instead of instancing the existing Mathlib version. Bridge lemmas needed to consume it are the symptom.
135	- **Suspect bodies on substantive claims**: `:= sorry` on a load-bearing claim, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial statement, `:= Classical.choice _` without authorization.
136	- **Axioms** on non-trivial claims that the project hasn't explicitly authorized in the strategy.
137	
138	A finding that meets any of the above lands at must-fix-this-iter — even if classifying it as "major" would feel more diplomatic. Soft severity is how wrong code hardens into the project.
139	
140	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>. Why must-fix: <one line>.
141	- ...
142	
143	## Major
144	
145	Findings that are real issues but don't meet the must-fix bar — typically stale comments that don't actively mislead, naming drift, minor code smells.
146	
147	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>.
148	- ...
149	
150	## Minor
151	
152	Low-impact observations.
153	
154	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>.
155	- ...
156	
157	## Excuse-comments (always called out separately)
158	
159	If any excuse-comments were flagged, list each one verbatim with its file:line. These deserve special visibility because they document the project lying to itself.
160	
161	- `Foo/Bar.lean:42`: "TODO: replace with the real definition" (attached to `Foo.bar`, which is a load-bearing definition in `Baz.lean`). Severity: critical.
162	- ...
163	
164	## Severity summary
165	
166	- **must-fix-this-iter**: <N> — these block downstream work in their files until addressed (see plan.md's per-file gate).
167	- **major**: <N>
168	- **minor**: <N>
169	- **excuse-comments**: <N> (also counted under must-fix-this-iter above; called out separately because they document the project lying to itself).
170	
171	Overall verdict: one sentence.
172	```
173	
174	**Strict severity reminder.** The plan agent's gate treats must-fix-this-iter as blocking. If you under-classify a wrong-decision finding to keep the loop's momentum, you are working against the project — wrong code accumulates faster than reviewers can catch it later. When in doubt, classify higher, not lower.
175	
176	## Return value
177	
178	Your final assistant message:
179	
180	- One line: `<slug>: <overall verdict> — <N> files audited, <M> issues (critical/major/minor: a/b/c)`
181	- The path to your full report.
182	
183	## Reminders
184	
185	- **Read every file. No scope shortcuts.**
186	- **No strategic context.** Your audit is unbiased by design.
187	- **You are read-only.** No project source, no blueprint, no state files (except your own report).
188	- **Excuse-comments are red flags.** Critical or major severity, every time.
189	- **Per-file checklist is the primary output.** Cover every file; the format is fixed.

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	Archon talks back to the user through **`TO_USER.md`** — a single, *persistent* notice board surfaced as a UI banner. It is **shared and writable by every agent** (plan, prover, review). It is no longer cleared at the start of each review phase; content survives across iters until an agent prunes it. The discipline every writer follows:
112	
113	- **Concise**: the whole file stays ≤ 2–3 short bullets. It is a banner, not a log.
114	- **Relevant now**: before adding anything, read the current content and **delete any bullet that is no longer true at this point** (the blocker was resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential got set). Never let it accumulate stale notices.
115	- **Notice board, never a question queue**: the loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. State decisions as *made* and blockers as *worked-around-as-far-as-possible*; the user steers asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md`.
116	
117	## Agent Roles
118	
119	### Plan Agent
120	
121	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
122	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
123	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
124	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
125	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
126	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
127	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.

129	### Prover Agent
130	
131	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
132	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
133	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
134	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
135	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
136	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
137	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.

139	### Subagents
140	
141	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).

149	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
150	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.

80	Goal: construct a cochain-complex isomorphism
81	  `Hom(K(𝒰)_•, F) ≅ Č•(𝒰, F)`
82	where:
83	  • `K(𝒰)_p = ∐_{σ : Fin(p+1) → 𝒰.I₀} freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)`
84	    (the degree-`p` term of `cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰`)

86	    (the degree-`p` term of `sectionCechComplex 𝒰 F`)

89	Step 1 — per-degree hom-coproduct duality:
90	  For each `p`, the Yoneda/free-presheaf adjunction gives
91	    `Hom(freeYoneda.obj V, F) ≅ F.obj V`  (natural in `V`).
92	  This is exactly `AlgebraicGeometry.freeYonedaHomAddEquiv` from `PresheafCech.lean`.
93	  Combined with the coproduct-hom identity
94	    `Hom(∐_σ A_σ, F) ≅ ∏_σ Hom(A_σ, F)`
95	  (via `preadditiveYoneda` preservation of limits, or hand-rolled from
96	  `Limits.Sigma.desc` / `Limits.Sigma.ι`), we get a degree-wise `Ab`-iso
97	    `Hom(K(𝒰)_p, F) ≅ ∏_σ F.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ) = Č^p(𝒰, F)`.

100	  Show that the degree-wise isos commute with the differentials.  The differential on
101	  `Hom(K(𝒰)_•, F)` is precomposition with the Čech boundary `d_p : K(𝒰)_{p+1} → K(𝒰)_p`
102	  (the alternating sum of face maps of `cechFreeSimplicial`).  The differential on
103	  `Č•(𝒰, F)` is the alternating sum of restriction maps built by `sectionCechComplex`.
104	  These match under the per-degree iso by naturality of `freeYonedaHomAddEquiv`.
105	
106	Step 3 — assemble:
107	  Use `HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents` (or equivalent) to combine the per-degree
108	  isos into a full cochain-complex iso, supplying the naturality squares from Step 2.

111	  • `AlgebraicGeometry.freeYonedaHomAddEquiv` (PresheafCech.lean): the per-degree iso.
112	  • `Limits.Sigma.desc`, `Limits.Sigma.ι`: coproduct universal property in

114	  • `HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents`: assemble component-wise isos into a

169	        (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => Opposite.op (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))))

176	    (Limits.Pi.mapIso e).hom = Limits.Pi.map (fun b => (e b).hom) := rfl

216	        = yoneda.map h ≫ PresheafOfModules.freeHomEquiv ψ from

289	private lemma homCechCosimplicial_δ (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
290	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) {n : ℕ} (i : Fin (n + 2)) :
291	    (homCechCosimplicial 𝒰 F).δ i

299	cochain-complex isomorphism `homCechComplexMapOpIso`. -/
300	private lemma homCechComplex_d_eq (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
301	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (p : ℕ) :
302	    (homCechComplex 𝒰 F).d p (p + 1)
303	      = (((preadditiveYoneda.obj F).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj
304	          (HomologicalComplex.op (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰))).d p (p + 1) := by
305	  have hL : (homCechComplex 𝒰 F).d p (p + 1)
306	      = AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (homCechCosimplicial 𝒰 F) p :=
307	    CochainComplex.of_d _ _ (AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.d_squared _) p
308	  have hR : (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).d (p + 1) p
309	      = AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰) p :=
310	    ChainComplex.of_d _ _ (AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.d_squared _) p
311	  rw [hL, AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD,
312	    Functor.mapHomologicalComplex_obj_d, HomologicalComplex.op_d, hR,
313	    AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD]
314	  have hop := CategoryTheory.op_sum
315	    ((cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰).obj (Opposite.op (SimplexCategory.mk (p + 1))))
316	    ((cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰).obj (Opposite.op (SimplexCategory.mk p)))
317	    (Finset.univ : Finset (Fin (p + 2)))
318	    (fun i => (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) • (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰).δ i)
319	  erw [hop, Functor.map_sum]
320	  refine Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => ?_)
321	  rw [homCechCosimplicial_δ, CategoryTheory.op_zsmul]
322	  erw [Functor.map_zsmul]
323	  rfl

325	/-- **Hom-complex as contravariant transport of the free Čech complex.**
326	
327	The cochain-complex isomorphism `Hom(K(𝒰)_•, F) ≅ Hom(-, F)((K(𝒰)_•)ᵒᵖ)` identifying the
328	alternating-coface hom-complex `homCechComplex 𝒰 F` with the contravariant transport
329	`(preadditiveYoneda.obj F).mapHomologicalComplex` of the opposite of the free Čech chain
330	complex `cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰`.  Degreewise the two complexes have the *same* term
331	(`Hom_{PMod}(K(𝒰)_p, F)`), so the components are identities; the differential squares are
332	`homCechComplex_d_eq`.
333	
334	This is the bridge that turns Lane-1's free resolution into Čech acyclicity: applying
335	`quasiIso_map_preadditiveYoneda_of_injective` to `(cechFreeComplexAug 𝒰).op` produces a
336	quasi-isomorphism of mapped-opposite complexes, and this iso transports it onto
337	`homCechComplex 𝒰 F` (hence, via `cechComplex_hom_identification`, onto `sectionCechComplex`).
338	
339	Project-local: the identification of the alternating-coface hom-complex with the mapped
340	opposite of the alternating-face free complex has no Mathlib counterpart. -/
341	noncomputable def homCechComplexMapOpIso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
342	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
343	    homCechComplex 𝒰 F ≅
344	      ((preadditiveYoneda.obj F).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj
345	        (HomologicalComplex.op (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰)) :=
346	  HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents (fun _ => Iso.refl _) (by
347	    rintro i j (rfl : i + 1 = j)
348	    exact (Category.id_comp _).trans
349	      ((homCechComplex_d_eq 𝒰 F i).symm.trans (Category.comp_id _).symm))
350	
351	/-- **Section Čech complex as contravariant transport of the free Čech complex.**
352	
353	Composing `homCechComplexMapOpIso` with the Čech hom-identification
354	`cechComplex_hom_identification` gives a cochain-complex isomorphism between the mapped
355	opposite of the free Čech complex and the section Čech complex `Č•(coverOpen 𝒰, F)`.  This is
356	the single isomorphism needed for the injective-acyclicity assembly: once Lane 1 provides
357	`QuasiIso (cechFreeComplexAug 𝒰)`, mapping its opposite through `preadditiveYoneda.obj F`
358	(quasi-iso by `quasiIso_map_preadditiveYoneda_of_injective`) and transporting the source/target
359	across this iso identifies `sectionCechComplex` with the mapped opposite of a complex
360	concentrated in degree `0`, giving positive-degree vanishing.
361	
362	Project-local: assembled from two project-local isomorphisms. -/
363	noncomputable def sectionCechComplexMapOpIso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
364	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
365	    ((preadditiveYoneda.obj F).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj
366	        (HomologicalComplex.op (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰))
367	      ≅ sectionCechComplex (coverOpen 𝒰) F :=
368	  (homCechComplexMapOpIso 𝒰 F).symm ≪≫ cechComplex_hom_identification 𝒰 F

370	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `Hom(-, I)` is exact for injective `I`
371	
372	The presheaf-level Čech-acyclicity argument (`lem:injective_cech_acyclic`) needs the single
373	categorical fact that, for an injective object `I` of an abelian category, the contravariant
374	hom functor `Hom(-, I)` is exact — equivalently, the covariant functor
375	`preadditiveYoneda.obj I : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab` preserves homology. Mathlib supplies the two pieces:
376	`preadditiveYoneda.obj I` always preserves finite limits (it is left exact), and injectivity
377	is equivalent to it preserving epimorphisms; we assemble these into preservation of finite
378	colimits (hence of homology), and feed the result through
379	`HomologicalComplex.quasiIsoAt_map_of_preservesHomology` to obtain quasi-isomorphism
380	preservation. These are stated for a general abelian category so they apply verbatim to
381	`X.PresheafOfModules` once the injectivity of `I` as a presheaf of modules is in hand.
382	
383	Project-local: Mathlib has the ingredients but not the packaged exactness of `Hom(-, I)` as a
384	homology-preserving functor. -/

386	section InjectiveHomExact
387	
388	open CategoryTheory Limits
389	
390	variable {C : Type*} [Category C] [Abelian C]
391	
392	/-- **`Hom(-, I)` preserves finite colimits for injective `I`.** For an injective object `I`
393	of an abelian category, the covariant functor `preadditiveYoneda.obj I : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab`
394	preserves finite colimits: it is left exact (preserves finite limits) automatically, and
395	injectivity makes it preserve epimorphisms, which together with left exactness gives
396	exactness. Combined with the automatic `PreservesFiniteLimits` instance this yields
397	`(preadditiveYoneda.obj I).PreservesHomology` by `Functor.preservesHomologyOfExact`.
398	
399	Project-local: the exactness of `Hom(-, I)` for injective `I` is the categorical engine of
400	`lem:injective_cech_acyclic`. -/
401	instance preadditiveYoneda_obj_preservesFiniteColimits_of_injective
402	    (I : C) [Injective I] : PreservesFiniteColimits (preadditiveYoneda.obj I) := by
403	  have hepi : (preadditiveYoneda.obj I).PreservesEpimorphisms :=
404	    (Injective.injective_iff_preservesEpimorphisms_preadditiveYoneda_obj I).mp inferInstance
405	  rw [Functor.preservesFiniteColimits_iff_forall_exact_map_and_epi]
406	  intro S hS
407	  have hg := hS.epi_g
408	  exact ⟨hS.exact.map_of_mono_of_preservesKernel _ hS.mono_f inferInstance,
409	    hepi.preserves S.g⟩
410	
411	/-- **`Hom(-, I)` carries quasi-isomorphisms to quasi-isomorphisms for injective `I`.**
412	Since `preadditiveYoneda.obj I` preserves homology
413	(`preadditiveYoneda_obj_preservesFiniteColimits_of_injective`), applying it degreewise to a
414	quasi-isomorphism `φ` of chain complexes in `Cᵒᵖ` produces a quasi-isomorphism of the
415	`Hom`-cochain complexes. This is the form consumed by `injective_cech_acyclic`: the
416	augmentation `cechFreeComplexAug 𝒰` (a quasi-isomorphism once Lane 1 lands), taken opposite
417	and mapped through `preadditiveYoneda.obj F`, stays a quasi-isomorphism.
418	
419	Project-local: packages `quasiIsoAt_map_of_preservesHomology` against the injective-hom
420	exactness instance above. -/
421	lemma quasiIso_map_preadditiveYoneda_of_injective (I : C) [Injective I]
422	    {ι : Type*} {c : ComplexShape ι} {K L : HomologicalComplex Cᵒᵖ c}
423	    (φ : K ⟶ L) [QuasiIso φ] :
424	    QuasiIso (((preadditiveYoneda.obj I).mapHomologicalComplex c).map φ) := by
425	  rw [quasiIso_iff]
426	  intro i
427	  exact HomologicalComplex.quasiIsoAt_map_of_preservesHomology φ (preadditiveYoneda.obj I) i
428	    (hφ := QuasiIso.quasiIsoAt (f := φ) i)
429	
430	end InjectiveHomExact

432	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Čech `H¹` vanishing ⟹ cocycles are coboundaries
433	
434	The surjectivity-on-sections step `ses_cech_h1` (blueprint `lem:ses_cech_h1`, Stacks
435	`lemma-ses-cech-h1`) consumes the {\v C}ech-algebra fact that a covering with
436	`Ȟ¹(𝒰, F) = 0` has *every* {\v C}ech `1`-cocycle equal to a coboundary. This is the
437	`\uses{def:cech_complex}` content of that lemma, isolated here as a self-contained
438	statement about the section {\v C}ech complex `sectionCechComplex` (no sheaf gluing or
439	local-surjectivity input). It is the converse direction of
440	`sectionCech_isZero_homology_of_objD_exact` (CechAcyclic), packaged in section
441	coordinates via `sectionCechProductEquiv` / `sectionCech_objD_apply`.
442	
443	Project-local: Mathlib has no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules, so
444	neither the homology-to-exactness reduction nor its coordinate form exist there. -/

446	section CechH1Coboundary
447	
448	open AlgebraicTopology
449	
450	/-- **Homology vanishing ⟹ coface-differential exactness** (converse of
451	`sectionCech_isZero_homology_of_objD_exact`). If the degree-`(q+1)` homology of the
452	section {\v C}ech complex vanishes, the underlying group homomorphisms of the two
453	consecutive coface differentials `objD q`, `objD (q+1)` form an exact sequence. Pure
454	homological algebra: the same `exactAt_iff_isZero_homology` / `exactAt_iff'` /
455	`ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact` chain, run in the extraction direction.
456	
457	Project-local: extraction form of the `Ab`-side homology bridge of
458	`lem:section_cech_homology_exact`, needed by the `ses_cech_h1` coboundary step. -/
459	theorem sectionCech_objD_exact_of_isZero_homology {ι : Type u}
460	    (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (q : ℕ)
461	    (h : IsZero ((sectionCechComplex U F).homology (q + 1))) :
462	    Function.Exact
463	      (ConcreteCategory.hom (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q))
464	      (ConcreteCategory.hom
465	        (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) (q + 1))) := by
466	  have hf : (sectionCechComplex U F).d q (q + 1)
467	      = AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q :=
468	    CochainComplex.of_d _ _ (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.d_squared _) q
469	  have hg : (sectionCechComplex U F).d (q + 1) (q + 2)
470	      = AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) (q + 1) :=
471	    CochainComplex.of_d _ _ (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.d_squared _) (q + 1)
472	  have key : Function.Exact
473	      (ConcreteCategory.hom ((sectionCechComplex U F).d q (q + 1)))
474	      (ConcreteCategory.hom ((sectionCechComplex U F).d (q + 1) (q + 2))) := by
475	    have hh := h
476	    rw [← HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff_isZero_homology,
477	        (sectionCechComplex U F).exactAt_iff' q (q + 1) (q + 2) (by simp) (by simp),
478	        ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact] at hh
479	    exact hh
480	  rw [hf, hg] at key
481	  exact key
482	
483	/-- **Čech `1`-cocycles are coboundaries when `Ȟ¹(𝒰, F) = 0`** — the
484	`\uses{def:cech_complex}` heart of `ses_cech_h1`.
485	
486	Working in the section coordinates of `sectionCechCosimplicial` (a family of sections
487	`c σ ∈ F(⨅ₖ U (σ k))` indexed by double-indices `σ : Fin 2 → ι`), if the family
488	satisfies the {\v C}ech `1`-cocycle identity (the alternating sum of its restrictions
489	over each triple-index `σ : Fin 3 → ι` vanishes) and the degree-`1` homology of the
490	section {\v C}ech complex vanishes, then `c` is a coboundary: there is a `0`-cochain
491	`t` (sections `t σ ∈ F(U (σ 0))` over single-indices) with `c σ` the alternating sum
492	of the face restrictions of `t`, i.e. `c σ = t_{σ 1}|_{σ} - t_{σ 0}|_{σ}` once the
493	two-term sum is expanded. The restriction maps are `sectionCechFaceRestr`, exactly the
494	{\v C}ech coface restrictions appearing in `sectionCech_objD_apply`.
495	
496	Project-local: the section-coordinate coboundary extraction for the section {\v C}ech
497	complex has no Mathlib counterpart. -/
498	theorem sectionCech_one_coboundary_of_isZero_homology {ι : Type u}
499	    (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.PresheafOfModules)
500	    (h : IsZero ((sectionCechComplex U F).homology 1))
501	    (c : ∀ σ : Fin 2 → ι, F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
502	    (hcoc : ∀ σ : Fin 3 → ι,
503	      ∑ i : Fin 3, (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
504	        ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U F σ i)
505	          (c (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)) = 0) :
506	    ∃ t : ∀ σ : Fin 1 → ι, F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))),
507	      ∀ σ : Fin 2 → ι, c σ = ∑ i : Fin 2, (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
508	        ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U F σ i)
509	          (t (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)) := by
510	  have he := sectionCech_objD_exact_of_isZero_homology U F 0 h
511	  set c' : ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial U F).obj (SimplexCategory.mk 1)) :=
512	    (sectionCechProductEquiv U F 1).symm c with hc'
513	  have hpe1 : sectionCechProductEquiv U F 1 c' = c :=
514	    (sectionCechProductEquiv U F 1).apply_symm_apply c
515	  have hzero : ConcreteCategory.hom
516	      (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) 1) c' = 0 := by
517	    apply (sectionCechProductEquiv U F 2).injective
518	    funext σ
519	    rw [sectionCech_objD_apply U F 1 c' σ, hpe1]
520	    rw [show sectionCechProductEquiv U F 2 0 σ = 0 from by
521	      rw [sectionCechProductEquiv_apply]; exact map_zero _]
522	    exact hcoc σ
523	  obtain ⟨b, hb⟩ := (he c').mp hzero
524	  refine ⟨sectionCechProductEquiv U F 0 b, fun σ => ?_⟩
525	  rw [← hpe1, ← hb, sectionCech_objD_apply U F 0 b σ]
526	
527	end CechH1Coboundary

529	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — surjectivity on sections from {\v C}ech-`H¹`
530	vanishing (`ses_cech_h1`)
531	
532	This section assembles the {\v C}ech-`H¹` vanishing into the surjectivity statement
533	`ses_cech_h1` (blueprint `lem:ses_cech_h1`, Stacks `lemma-ses-cech-h1`).  The
534	{\v C}ech-algebra heart is `sectionCech_one_coboundary_of_isZero_homology` (the
535	cocycle-to-coboundary extraction proven above); the remaining content is the standard
536	sheaf theory — local lifting, left-exactness on sections, and gluing — which here is
537	supplied through Mathlib's `TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf.isSheafUniqueGluing` and a handful of
538	restriction-bookkeeping helpers.

540	Project-local: Mathlib has no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules, so
541	the surjectivity criterion phrased through `sectionCechComplex` is new here. -/
542	
543	section SesCechH1
544	
545	open AlgebraicTopology TopologicalSpace Opposite
546	
547	/-- **Composite of two presheaf restrictions is the direct restriction.**
548	For `A ≤ B ≤ C` the restriction `P(C) → P(B)` followed by `P(B) → P(A)` equals the direct
549	restriction `P(C) → P(A)`.  Holds definitionally for `Ab`-valued presheaves on opens
550	because the composite of the opposite `homOfLE`s is the opposite `homOfLE` of the
551	composite.  Used pervasively to collapse restriction chains in `ses_cech_h1`. -/
552	private lemma restr_trans (P : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab.{u}) {A B C : Opens ↥X}
553	    (h1 : A ≤ B) (h2 : B ≤ C) (x : ToType (P.obj (op C))) :
554	    ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map (homOfLE h1).op)
555	        (ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map (homOfLE h2).op) x)
556	      = ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map (homOfLE (h1.trans h2)).op) x := by
557	  rw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← P.map_comp, ← op_comp]
558	  rfl
559	
560	/-- **Restriction between two equal opens is injective.**  When `V ≤ W` and `W ≤ V`
561	(i.e. `V = W`) the restriction `P(W) → P(V)` is injective: the reverse restriction is a
562	two-sided inverse (`restr_trans` collapses the round trip to the identity, by uniqueness of
563	poset morphisms).  Used to transport the {\v C}ech compatibility equation between the binary
564	intersection `U i ⊓ U j` and the {\v C}ech double-overlap `⨅ₖ U (![i,j] k)`. -/
565	private lemma restr_inj_of_eq (P : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab.{u}) {V W : Opens ↥X}
566	    (h : V ≤ W) (h' : W ≤ V) :
567	    Function.Injective (ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map (homOfLE h).op)) := by
568	  intro a b hab
569	  have h2 := congrArg (ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map (homOfLE h').op)) hab
570	  rw [restr_trans, restr_trans] at h2
571	  rwa [Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE (h'.trans h)) (𝟙 _), op_id, P.map_id,
572	    ConcreteCategory.id_apply, ConcreteCategory.id_apply] at h2
573	
574	/-- **Two parallel restrictions agree.**  Any two restriction maps `P(A) → P(C)` (opposite
575	opens morphisms) act identically, by uniqueness of poset morphisms.  Used to discharge the
576	final defeq-up-to-proof-irrelevance step after collapsing restriction chains with
577	`← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← Functor.map_comp`. -/
578	private lemma restr_op_unique (P : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab.{u}) {A C : Opens ↥X}
579	    (f g : Opposite.op C ⟶ Opposite.op A) (x : ToType (P.obj (Opposite.op C))) :
580	    ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map f) x = ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map g) x := by
581	  rw [show f = g from Quiver.Hom.unop_inj (Subsingleton.elim _ _)]
582	
583	/-- **Restriction of a single-index {\v C}ech section is invariant under reindexing the
584	tuple.**  For two equal `Fin 1`-tuples `ρ = ρ'` (e.g. `fun _ => i` and `![i,j] ∘ δ`), the
585	restrictions of `g' ρ` and `g' ρ'` to a common open `W` agree.  Proved by `subst` (so the
586	propositional-but-not-definitional tuple equality is discharged without a heterogeneous
587	rewrite), then `restr_op_unique`.  This is the bridge between the `U`-indexed glue family
588	(`fun _ => i`) and the {\v C}ech double-overlap agreement (`![i,j] ∘ δ`). -/
589	private lemma restr_g'_transport {ι : Type u} (U : ι → Opens ↥X) (G : X.PresheafOfModules)
590	    (g' : ∀ ρ : Fin 1 → ι, ToType (G.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (ρ k)))))
591	    {W : Opens ↥X} {ρ ρ' : Fin 1 → ι} (hρ : ρ = ρ')
592	    (f : Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (ρ k)) ⟶ Opposite.op W)
593	    (f' : Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (ρ' k)) ⟶ Opposite.op W) :
594	    ConcreteCategory.hom (G.presheaf.map f) (g' ρ)
595	      = ConcreteCategory.hom (G.presheaf.map f') (g' ρ') := by
596	  subst hρ
597	  exact restr_op_unique G.presheaf f f' (g' ρ)
598	
599	/-- **A morphism of presheaves of modules commutes with the {\v C}ech face restrictions.**
600	For `fι : F.presheaf ⟶ G.presheaf` the section-level map `fι.app` intertwines the
601	{\v C}ech face restriction `sectionCechFaceRestr` of `F` with that of `G`; this is just
602	the naturality of `fι`.  It is the single naturality input that pushes the cocycle and
603	coboundary identities of the heart between `F` and `G`. -/
604	private lemma fι_sectionCechFaceRestr {ι : Type u} (U : ι → Opens ↥X)
605	    (F G : X.PresheafOfModules) (fι : F.presheaf ⟶ G.presheaf)
606	    {q : ℕ} (σ : Fin (q + 2) → ι) (i : Fin (q + 2))
607	    (x : ToType (F.presheaf.obj
608	      (op (⨅ l, U ((σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom) l))))) :
609	    ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U G σ i)
610	        (ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app _) x)
611	      = ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app _)
612	          (ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U F σ i) x) := by
613	  unfold sectionCechFaceRestr
614	  rw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, fι.naturality]
615	
616	/-- The single-index {\v C}ech intersection at a constant tuple is the cover member. -/
617	private lemma coverConst_iInf {ι : Type u} (U : ι → Opens ↥X) (i : ι) :
618	    (⨅ k : Fin 1, U ((fun _ => i) k)) = U i := by simp
619	
620	/-- The double-index {\v C}ech intersection at `![i, j]` is the binary intersection. -/
621	private lemma coverPair_iInf {ι : Type u} (U : ι → Opens ↥X) (i j : ι) :
622	    (⨅ k : Fin 2, U (![i, j] k)) = U i ⊓ U j := by
623	  apply le_antisymm
624	  · exact le_inf (iInf_le _ 0) (iInf_le _ 1)
625	  · exact le_iInf (fun k => by fin_cases k <;> simp)

628	private lemma pair_comp_δ0 {ι : Type u} (i j : ι) :
629	    (![i, j] ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ (0 : Fin 2)).toOrderHom) = (fun _ => j) := by
630	  funext k; fin_cases k; simp [SimplexCategory.δ, Fin.succAbove]
631	
632	/-- The `1`-st face of `![i, j]` is the constant tuple `i`. -/
633	private lemma pair_comp_δ1 {ι : Type u} (i j : ι) :
634	    (![i, j] ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ (1 : Fin 2)).toOrderHom) = (fun _ => i) := by
635	  funext k; fin_cases k; simp [SimplexCategory.δ, Fin.succAbove]
636	
637	set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
638	/-- **Surjectivity on sections from {\v C}ech-`H¹` vanishing** (blueprint `lem:ses_cech_h1`,
639	Stacks `lemma-ses-cech-h1`).
640	
641	For a short exact sequence of presheaves of modules `0 → F → G → H → 0` on a scheme `X`
642	— presented through the underlying `Ab`-presheaf maps `fι`, `gπ`, with `fι` a monomorphism
643	on sections (`hmono`), `gπ ∘ fι = 0` (`hπι`), and every kernel section of `gπ` in the image
644	of `fι` (`hker`) — together with an open cover `U` of the open `⨆ᵢ Uᵢ` such that
645	`Ȟ¹(U, F) = 0` (`hH1`) and a family of local lifts `sLoc i ∈ G(Uᵢ)` of `s ∈ H(⨆ᵢ Uᵢ)`
646	(`hlift`), the section `s` lies in the image of `G(⨆ᵢ Uᵢ) → H(⨆ᵢ Uᵢ)`.
647	
648	The differences `sⱼ|_{ij} − sᵢ|_{ij}` of the local lifts map to `0` in `H`, hence lift to a
649	{\v C}ech `1`-cocycle in `F` (`hker`); the {\v C}ech-`H¹` vanishing makes it a coboundary
650	(`sectionCech_one_coboundary_of_isZero_homology`); subtracting the coboundary makes the
651	corrected lifts agree on overlaps, and the sheaf condition on `G` glues them to a global
652	section of `G` over `⨆ᵢ Uᵢ` mapping to `s` (separatedness of `H` identifies its image with
653	`s`).  The "cofinal system of covers" of the Stacks statement is captured here by taking a
654	single cover satisfying both `Ȟ¹ = 0` and the local-lift property as hypotheses.
655	
656	Project-local: the {\v C}ech surjectivity criterion phrased through `sectionCechComplex` is
657	new here (Mathlib has no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules). -/
658	theorem ses_cech_h1 {ι : Type u} (U : ι → Opens ↥X)
659	    (F G H : X.PresheafOfModules)
660	    (fι : F.presheaf ⟶ G.presheaf) (gπ : G.presheaf ⟶ H.presheaf)
661	    (hGsh : TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf (X := X.toTopCat) G.presheaf)
662	    (hHsh : TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf (X := X.toTopCat) H.presheaf)
663	    (hπι : ∀ (V : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ) (x : ToType (F.presheaf.obj V)),
664	      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app V) (ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app V) x) = 0)
665	    (hmono : ∀ (V : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ), Function.Injective (ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app V)))
666	    (hker : ∀ (V : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ) (x : ToType (G.presheaf.obj V)),
667	      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app V) x = 0 → ∃ y, ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app V) y = x)
668	    (s : ToType (H.presheaf.obj (op (iSup U))))
669	    (hH1 : IsZero ((sectionCechComplex U F).homology 1))
670	    (sLoc : ∀ i, ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (U i))))
671	    (hlift : ∀ i, ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (U i))) (sLoc i)
672	      = ConcreteCategory.hom (H.presheaf.map (Opens.leSupr U i).op) s) :
673	    ∃ g : ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (iSup U))),
674	      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (iSup U))) g = s := by
675	  classical
676	  -- single-index local sections, recast in ⨅-coordinates
677	  set sLoc'coord : (σ : Fin 1 → ι) → ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) :=
678	    fun σ => ConcreteCategory.hom (G.presheaf.map (homOfLE (iInf_le _ 0)).op) (sLoc (σ 0))
679	    with hsLoc'coord
680	  set sLoc' : ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial U G).obj (SimplexCategory.mk 0)) :=
681	    (sectionCechProductEquiv U G 0).symm sLoc'coord with hsLoc'
682	  have hpe0 : sectionCechProductEquiv U G 0 sLoc' = sLoc'coord :=
683	    (sectionCechProductEquiv U G 0).apply_symm_apply sLoc'coord
684	  -- the degree-1 cochain of G-differences
685	  set cyc1 := ConcreteCategory.hom
686	    (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U G) 0) sLoc' with hcyc1
687	  set dGcoord : (σ : Fin 2 → ι) → ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) :=
688	    fun σ => sectionCechProductEquiv U G 1 cyc1 σ with hdGcoord
689	  have hdG : ∀ σ : Fin 2 → ι, dGcoord σ
690	      = ∑ i : Fin 2, (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
691	          ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U G σ i)
692	            (sLoc'coord (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)) := by
693	    intro σ
694	    have happ := sectionCech_objD_apply U G 0 sLoc' σ
695	    rw [hpe0] at happ
696	    exact happ
697	  -- `gπ` carries a single ⨅-coordinate local section to the restriction of `s`
698	  have hgπsLoc' : ∀ (ρ : Fin 1 → ι) (h : (⨅ k, U (ρ k)) ≤ iSup U),
699	      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (⨅ k, U (ρ k)))) (sLoc'coord ρ)
700	        = ConcreteCategory.hom (H.presheaf.map (homOfLE h).op) s := by
701	    intro ρ h
702	    rw [hsLoc'coord, ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, gπ.naturality,
703	      ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, hlift (ρ 0)]
704	    exact restr_trans H.presheaf (iInf_le _ 0) (le_iSup U (ρ 0)) s
705	  -- `gπ` kills the differences
706	  have hgπdG : ∀ σ : Fin 2 → ι,
707	      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) (dGcoord σ) = 0 := by
708	    intro σ
709	    rw [hdG, map_sum]
710	    have key : ∀ i : Fin 2,
711	        ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
712	          ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) • ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U G σ i)
713	            (sLoc'coord (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)))
714	        = (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) • ConcreteCategory.hom (H.presheaf.map
715	            (homOfLE (le_trans (iInf_le (fun k => U (σ k)) (0 : Fin 2)) (le_iSup U (σ 0)))).op) s := by
716	      intro i
717	      rw [map_zsmul]; congr 1
718	      rw [← fι_sectionCechFaceRestr U G H gπ σ i,
719	        hgπsLoc' (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom) (le_trans (iInf_le _ 0) (le_iSup U _))]
720	      simp only [sectionCechFaceRestr, ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← Functor.map_comp]
721	      exact restr_op_unique H.presheaf _ _ s
722	    rw [Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => key i), ← Finset.sum_smul,
723	      show (∑ i : Fin 2, (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ)) = 0 by decide, zero_smul]
724	  -- lift each difference into `F`
725	  have hc : ∀ σ : Fin 2 → ι, ∃ y : ToType (F.presheaf.obj (op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))),
726	      ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app _) y = dGcoord σ :=
727	    fun σ => hker _ _ (hgπdG σ)
728	  choose c hcspec using hc
729	  -- the `G`-side cocycle identity (`d² = 0`)
730	  have hGcoc : ∀ σ : Fin 3 → ι, ∑ i : Fin 3, (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
731	      ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U G σ i)
732	        (dGcoord (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)) = 0 := by
733	    intro σ
734	    have happ := sectionCech_objD_apply U G 1 cyc1 σ
735	    simp only [hdGcoord]
736	    rw [← happ]
737	    have hzero : ConcreteCategory.hom
738	        (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U G) 1) cyc1 = 0 := by
739	      rw [hcyc1, ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.d_squared]
740	      simp
741	    rw [hzero, sectionCechProductEquiv_apply]; exact map_zero _
742	  -- the `F`-side cocycle identity for the lifted differences
743	  have hcoc : ∀ σ : Fin 3 → ι, ∑ i : Fin 3, (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
744	      ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U F σ i)
745	        (c (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)) = 0 := by
746	    intro σ
747	    apply hmono (op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))
748	    rw [map_sum, map_zero]
749	    rw [show (0 : ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))) = _ from (hGcoc σ).symm]
750	    refine Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => ?_)
751	    rw [map_zsmul, ← fι_sectionCechFaceRestr U F G fι σ i, hcspec]
752	  -- coboundary extraction (the {\v C}ech-`H¹` heart)
753	  obtain ⟨t, ht⟩ := sectionCech_one_coboundary_of_isZero_homology U F hH1 c hcoc
754	  -- the corrected family in ⨅-coordinates
755	  set g'coord : (ρ : Fin 1 → ι) → ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (⨅ k, U (ρ k)))) :=
756	    fun ρ => sLoc'coord ρ - ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app _) (t ρ) with hg'coord
757	  have hg'coc : ∀ σ : Fin 2 → ι, ∑ i : Fin 2, (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
758	      ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U G σ i)
759	        (g'coord (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)) = 0 := by
760	    intro σ
761	    simp only [hg'coord, map_sub, smul_sub, Finset.sum_sub_distrib, ← hdG]
762	    rw [← hcspec σ, ht σ, map_sum, sub_eq_zero]
763	    refine Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => ?_)
764	    rw [map_zsmul, ← fι_sectionCechFaceRestr U F G fι σ i]
765	  -- pairwise agreement of the corrected family on double overlaps
766	  have hagree2 : ∀ σ : Fin 2 → ι,
767	      ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U G σ 0)
768	        (g'coord (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ 0).toOrderHom))
769	      = ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U G σ 1)
770	        (g'coord (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ 1).toOrderHom)) := by
771	    intro σ
772	    have h := hg'coc σ
773	    rw [Fin.sum_univ_two] at h
774	    simp only [Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_one, pow_zero, one_smul, pow_one, neg_one_zsmul] at h
775	    rwa [add_neg_eq_zero] at h
776	  -- the `U`-indexed corrected family
777	  set gGlue : ∀ i, ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (U i))) :=
778	    fun i => ConcreteCategory.hom
779	      (G.presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_of_eq (coverConst_iInf U i).symm)).op) (g'coord (fun _ => i))
780	    with hgGlue
781	  have hcompat : TopCat.Presheaf.IsCompatible (X := X.toTopCat) G.presheaf U gGlue := by
782	    intro i j
783	    apply restr_inj_of_eq G.presheaf (le_of_eq (coverPair_iInf U i j))
784	      (le_of_eq (coverPair_iInf U i j).symm)
785	    simp only [hgGlue]
786	    erw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply,
787	      ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← G.presheaf.map_comp,
788	      ← G.presheaf.map_comp, ← G.presheaf.map_comp, ← G.presheaf.map_comp]
789	    have key := hagree2 ![i, j]
790	    simp only [sectionCechFaceRestr] at key
791	    exact (restr_g'_transport U G g'coord (pair_comp_δ1 i j).symm _ _).trans
792	      (key.symm.trans (restr_g'_transport U G g'coord (pair_comp_δ0 i j).symm _ _).symm)
793	  -- glue the corrected family to a global section of `G`
794	  obtain ⟨g, hgluing, -⟩ := hGsh.isSheafUniqueGluing U gGlue hcompat
795	  refine ⟨g, ?_⟩
796	  -- `gπ g = s` by separatedness of `H`
797	  set sH : ∀ i, ToType (H.presheaf.obj (op (U i))) :=
798	    fun i => ConcreteCategory.hom (H.presheaf.map (Opens.leSupr U i).op) s with hsH
799	  have hcompatH : TopCat.Presheaf.IsCompatible (X := X.toTopCat) H.presheaf U sH := by
800	    intro i j
801	    simp only [hsH]
802	    erw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← H.presheaf.map_comp,
803	      ← H.presheaf.map_comp]
804	    exact restr_op_unique H.presheaf _ _ s
805	  have hg2 : TopCat.Presheaf.IsGluing (X := X.toTopCat) H.presheaf U sH s := fun i => rfl
806	  have hgπglue : ∀ i, ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (U i))) (gGlue i) = sH i := by
807	    intro i
808	    simp only [hgGlue, hsH, hg'coord]
809	    rw [show Opens.leSupr U i = homOfLE (le_iSup U i) from rfl,
810	      ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, gπ.naturality, ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, map_sub,
811	      hπι, sub_zero, hgπsLoc' _ ((coverConst_iInf U i).le.trans (le_iSup U i)), restr_trans]
812	  have hg1 : TopCat.Presheaf.IsGluing (X := X.toTopCat) H.presheaf U sH
813	      (ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (iSup U))) g) := by
814	    intro i
815	    have hn : ConcreteCategory.hom (H.presheaf.map (Opens.leSupr U i).op)
816	        (ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (iSup U))) g)
817	      = ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (U i))) (gGlue i) := by
818	      rw [← hgluing i]
819	      erw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← gπ.naturality (Opens.leSupr U i).op,
820	        ConcreteCategory.comp_apply]
821	    show ConcreteCategory.hom (H.presheaf.map (Opens.leSupr U i).op)
822	        (ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (iSup U))) g) = sH i
823	    rw [hn]
824	    exact hgπglue i
825	  exact (hHsh.isSheafUniqueGluing U sH hcompatH).unique hg1 hg2
826	
827	end SesCechH1

829	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — injective {\v C}ech-acyclicity
830	
831	The final bridge step `injective_cech_acyclic` (blueprint `lem:injective_cech_acyclic`,
832	Stacks `lemma-injective-trivial-cech`): for an injective `O_X`-module `I` and any finite
833	open cover `𝒰`, the section {\v C}ech cohomology of `I` vanishes in positive degrees.  This
834	is the one-step op-transport assembly of the two lanes that landed in iter-024:
835	
836	- **Part 1** — `I` is injective as a *presheaf* of modules (`injective_toPresheafOfModules`,
837	  PresheafCech), so `Hom(-, I) = preadditiveYoneda.obj I` is exact
838	  (`quasiIso_map_preadditiveYoneda_of_injective`, above).
839	- **Part 2** — the free {\v C}ech complex is a resolution of `O_𝒰`: the augmentation
840	  `cechFreeComplexAug 𝒰` is a quasi-isomorphism (`cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`,
841	  FreePresheafComplex).
842	
843	Taking the opposite of the augmentation and applying `preadditiveYoneda.obj I` keeps it a
844	quasi-isomorphism (Part 1); transporting source and target across `sectionCechComplexMapOpIso`
845	identifies it with the section {\v C}ech complex of `I` mapped against the
846	degree-`0`-concentrated `O_𝒰[0]`, whose positive-degree homology vanishes objectwise.
847	
848	Project-local: the {\v C}ech-acyclicity of injective sheaves phrased through
849	`sectionCechComplex` has no Mathlib counterpart. -/
850	
851	set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
852	-- The op-transport assembly elaborates several nested functor-on-homological-complex
853	-- coercions whose defeq checks exceed the default heartbeat budget.
854	/-- **Injective sheaves are {\v C}ech-acyclic** (blueprint `lem:injective_cech_acyclic`,
855	Stacks `lemma-injective-trivial-cech`).
856	
857	For an injective `O_X`-module `I` and any finite open cover `𝒰` of (an open of) `X`, the
858	section {\v C}ech cohomology of `I` vanishes in every positive degree `p > 0`:
859	`Ȟᵖ(𝒰, I) = 0`.  Concretely the degree-`p` homology of the section {\v C}ech complex of the
860	underlying presheaf of modules `Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X |>.obj I` is a zero
861	object.
862	
863	The proof is the one-step op-transport assembly: the free {\v C}ech resolution
864	`cechFreeComplexAug 𝒰` is a quasi-isomorphism (`cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`); since `I` is
865	injective as a presheaf of modules (`injective_toPresheafOfModules`), applying
866	`preadditiveYoneda.obj I` to its opposite preserves the quasi-isomorphism
867	(`quasiIso_map_preadditiveYoneda_of_injective`); transporting across
868	`sectionCechComplexMapOpIso` identifies the section {\v C}ech complex with the mapped
869	opposite of a degree-`0`-concentrated complex, whose positive-degree homology vanishes.
870	
871	Project-local: Mathlib has no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules. -/
872	theorem injective_cech_acyclic (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (I : X.Modules) [Injective I]
873	    (p : ℕ) (hp : 0 < p) :
874	    IsZero ((sectionCechComplex (coverOpen 𝒰)
875	      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj I)).homology p) := by
876	  haveI : QuasiIso (cechFreeComplexAug 𝒰) := cechFreeComplex_quasiIso 𝒰
877	  haveI : Injective ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj I) :=
878	    injective_toPresheafOfModules I
879	  set F := (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj I with hF
880	  -- map the opposite of the augmentation through `Hom(-, I)`; quasi-iso preserved (Part 1)
881	  set ψ := ((preadditiveYoneda.obj F).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.down ℕ).symm).map
882	      ((HomologicalComplex.opFunctor X.PresheafOfModules (ComplexShape.down ℕ)).map
883	        (cechFreeComplexAug 𝒰).op) with hψdef
884	  haveI : QuasiIso ψ := quasiIso_map_preadditiveYoneda_of_injective F _
885	  -- transport the target onto the section Čech complex of `I`
886	  set Θ := ψ ≫ (sectionCechComplexMapOpIso 𝒰 F).hom with hΘdef

6	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Tilde
7	
8	/-!
9	# Quasi-coherent sheaves on an affine are sections-tilde (Stacks 01HV/01I8)
10	
11	Project-local: the affine quasi-coherent structure theorem.  For an `𝒪_X`-module `F`
12	on an affine `X = Spec R`, with `M = Γ(X, F)`, there is a natural isomorphism
13	`F ≅ M^~`, under which `Γ(D(f), F) = M_f`.
14	
15	## The Mathlib gradient
16	
17	Mathlib's `AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Tilde` development provides:
18	
19	* `Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ F : tilde (Γ F) ⟶ F` — the counit of the
20	  tilde ⊣ global-sections adjunction;
21	* `isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff : IsIso F.fromTildeΓ ↔ (tilde.functor R).essImage F`;
22	* `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation F (P : F.Presentation) : IsIso F.fromTildeΓ` —
23	  the counit is an isomorphism whenever `F` admits a **global** presentation
24	  (a global generating family together with a global generating family of relations).
25	
26	The genuine remaining gap — **Stacks Tag 01I8**, the affine equivalence
27	`QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R` — is the implication
28	
29	  `[IsQuasicoherent F]  ⟹  IsIso F.fromTildeΓ`   (on the affine `Spec R`).
30	
31	`IsQuasicoherent F` only supplies *local* presentation data on a cover
32	(`QuasicoherentData`); turning that into a *global* presentation on the affine base
33	(or directly into membership of the essential image of `tilde`) is the content of the
34	affine equivalence and is not yet in Mathlib.  See the `## Handoff` section at the
35	bottom of this file for the precise decomposition.
36	
37	This file therefore delivers the structure theorem **conditioned on the counit being
38	an isomorphism** (`qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`), and a ready-to-use **presentation form**
39	(`qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_of_presentation`) that discharges that condition via the
40	Mathlib presentation lemma.  Once the 01I8 instance
41	`[IsQuasicoherent F] → IsIso F.fromTildeΓ` lands, the conditional form upgrades to the
42	unconditional quasi-coherent statement with no further work.
43	-/
44	
45	universe u
46	
47	open CategoryTheory Limits
48	
49	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
50	
51	variable {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
52	
53	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — affine quasi-coherent structure theorem -/
54	
55	/-- **Affine structure theorem, conditional form (Stacks 01HV).**  If the tilde–Γ counit
56	`tilde (Γ F) ⟶ F` of an `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module `F` is an isomorphism — which holds for every
57	quasi-coherent `F` (the 01I8 globalisation `[IsQuasicoherent F] → IsIso F.fromTildeΓ` is the
58	sole remaining gap; see `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_of_presentation` for the presentation-based
59	discharge) — then `F` is isomorphic to the sheaf associated with its module of global
60	sections `M = Γ(Spec R, F)`.  Project-local because Mathlib exposes only the counit and the
61	`IsIso`-criterion, not this packaged `F ≅ M^~` form. -/
62	noncomputable def qcoh_iso_tilde_sections (F : (Spec R).Modules) [IsIso F.fromTildeΓ] :
63	    F ≅ tilde (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj F) :=
64	  (asIso F.fromTildeΓ).symm
65	
66	/-- **Affine structure theorem, presentation form (Stacks 01HV).**  An `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module
67	`F` that admits a *global* presentation (`F.Presentation`) is isomorphic to the sheaf
68	associated with its module of global sections `M = Γ(Spec R, F)`.  This discharges the
69	`IsIso F.fromTildeΓ` hypothesis of `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections` via Mathlib's
70	`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`.  Project-local for the same packaging reason. -/
71	noncomputable def qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_of_presentation (F : (Spec R).Modules)
72	    (P : F.Presentation) : F ≅ tilde (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj F) :=
73	  haveI := isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation F P
74	  (asIso F.fromTildeΓ).symm
75	
76	/-- The hom of `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections` is the inverse of the tilde–Γ counit. -/
77	@[simp]
78	lemma qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_hom (F : (Spec R).Modules) [IsIso F.fromTildeΓ] :
79	    (qcoh_iso_tilde_sections F).hom = inv F.fromTildeΓ :=
80	  rfl
81	
82	/-- The inverse of `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections` is the tilde–Γ counit `tilde (Γ F) ⟶ F`. -/
83	@[simp]
84	lemma qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_inv (F : (Spec R).Modules) [IsIso F.fromTildeΓ] :
85	    (qcoh_iso_tilde_sections F).inv = F.fromTildeΓ :=
86	  rfl

88	/-! ### Reduction to global generation (Stacks 01I8, steps 2–3)
89

156	      x ∈ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen g ∧ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen g ≤ U i := by
157	    intro x
158	    have hxtop : x ∈ (⊤ : (Spec R).Opens) := trivial
159	    rw [← hU] at hxtop
160	    obtain ⟨i, hi⟩ := TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.1 hxtop
161	    obtain ⟨V, hV, hxV, hVU⟩ :=
162	      (TopologicalSpace.Opens.isBasis_iff_nbhd.1
163	        (PrimeSpectrum.isBasis_basic_opens (R := R))) hi
164	    obtain ⟨g, rfl⟩ := hV
165	    exact ⟨g, i, hxV, hVU⟩
166	  choose g φ' hxg hgU using hpt
167	  -- quasicompactness: extract a finite subcover of the pointwise basic opens
168	  have hcover : (Set.univ : Set (PrimeSpectrum R)) ⊆
169	      ⋃ x, (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g x) : Set (PrimeSpectrum R)) :=
170	    fun x _ => Set.mem_iUnion.2 ⟨x, hxg x⟩
171	  obtain ⟨t, ht⟩ := isCompact_univ.elim_finite_subcover
172	    (fun x => (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g x) : Set (PrimeSpectrum R)))
173	    (fun x => (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g x)).isOpen) hcover

175	  refine ⟨t.card, fun j => g (e.symm j).val, fun j => φ' (e.symm j).val, fun j => hgU _, ?_⟩

178	  intro x _

180	  have hxu := ht (Set.mem_univ x)
181	  rw [Set.mem_iUnion₂] at hxu
182	  obtain ⟨y, hy, hxy⟩ := hxu
183	  refine ⟨e ⟨y, hy⟩, ?_⟩
